description: 100-case L1 routing accuracy test suite for ltc-insight-router

test_cases.description: 3 bed exits during night — clear sleep issue
test_cases.notes: 3 exits well above baseline avg of 0.5. Multi-day trend assumed established from prior days.

test_cases.description: Exactly 2 bed exits at night — threshold boundary
test_cases.notes: Boundary case: exactly 2 exits meets >= 2 threshold.

test_cases.description: 5 bed exits across two consecutive nights
test_cases.notes: Multi-day trend across 2 nights. Both nights exceed threshold.

test_cases.description: 2 bed exits at 23:00-23:59 window — night boundary
test_cases.notes: 23:00 is within the night window (23:00-06:00). Should trigger.

test_cases.description: 4 bed exits in early morning hours — severe pattern
test_cases.notes: 4 exits far exceeds threshold. Significant deviation from baseline.

test_cases.description: 1 bed exit at night — below threshold
test_cases.notes: Only 1 exit, below >= 2 threshold. No route.

test_cases.description: 0 bed exits at night — no events
test_cases.notes: No events at all. No route.

test_cases.description: 2 bed exits during daytime — not night window
test_cases.notes: Daytime bed exits (10:00, 14:30) are outside 23:00-06:00 night window. No sleep issue.

test_cases.description: 45-minute tossing and turning — above threshold
test_cases.notes: 45 min well above 30 min threshold and > 2 sigma from baseline.

test_cases.description: Exactly 30 minutes tossing and turning — boundary
test_cases.notes: Boundary case: exactly 30 minutes meets >= 30 threshold.

test_cases.description: 60-minute tossing and turning — severe
test_cases.notes: 60 min is extreme. Clear routing.

test_cases.description: Two nights with 35+ min tossing — multi-day trend
test_cases.notes: Multi-day trend of prolonged tossing. Both nights above threshold.

test_cases.description: Tossing 38 min across three nights — established trend
test_cases.notes: Three-night trend exceeding threshold every night. Strong routing signal.

test_cases.description: 15 minutes tossing — below threshold
test_cases.notes: 15 min is below 30 min threshold. No route.

test_cases.description: 25 minutes tossing — below threshold
test_cases.notes: 25 min is below 30 min threshold. No route.

test_cases.description: 29 minutes tossing — just below threshold
test_cases.notes: 29 min is just below 30 min threshold. No route.

test_cases.description: Combined: 2 bed exits + 35 min tossing same night
test_cases.notes: Both bed_exit and tossing thresholds exceeded. Routes to sleep expert once.

test_cases.description: Combined: 3 bed exits + 50 min tossing over two nights
test_cases.notes: Multi-day combined sleep disruption pattern. Strong signal.

test_cases.description: Combined: 1 bed exit + 32 min tossing — only tossing triggers
test_cases.notes: Bed exit count (1) below threshold but tossing (32 min) exceeds threshold. Routes on tossing.

test_cases.description: Combined: 4 bed exits + 55 min tossing — severe combined disruption
test_cases.notes: Severe combined pattern. Both thresholds exceeded by wide margins.

test_cases.description: Walking 35% below baseline — above mobility threshold
test_cases.notes: 0.52 is 35% below 0.80 baseline. Exceeds 30% threshold.

test_cases.description: Walking 40% below baseline — clear gait slowdown
test_cases.notes: 0.48 is 40% below 0.80 baseline. Clear mobility concern.

test_cases.description: Walking 50% below baseline over two days — severe trend
test_cases.notes: Multi-day 50% slowdown. Severe mobility degradation.

test_cases.description: Walking exactly 30% below baseline — boundary
test_cases.notes: Boundary case: exactly 30% below baseline (0.56 = 0.80 * 0.70). Meets threshold.

test_cases.description: Walking 33% below baseline — three-day trend
test_cases.notes: Three-day deteriorating gait trend. Progressive slowdown from 33% to 37.5%.

test_cases.description: Walking 20% below baseline — below threshold
test_cases.notes: 20% below baseline (0.64) does not meet 30% threshold. No route.

test_cases.description: Walking 15% below baseline — well within normal variation
test_cases.notes: 15% below baseline. Normal daily variation. No route.

test_cases.description: Walking 28% below baseline — just below threshold
test_cases.notes: 28% below baseline. Just shy of 30% threshold. No route.

test_cases.description: 2 consecutive rise attempt failures
test_cases.notes: 2 consecutive failures meets >= 2 threshold.

test_cases.description: 3 consecutive rise attempt failures — severe
test_cases.notes: 3 consecutive failures. Severe difficulty rising.

test_cases.description: Rise attempt failures across two mornings
test_cases.notes: Multi-day rise attempt failures. Clear trend.

test_cases.description: 4 consecutive rise attempt failures — very severe
test_cases.notes: 4 consecutive failures. Very severe rising difficulty.

test_cases.description: Rise attempt fails across three days
test_cases.notes: Three-day trend of consecutive rise failures every morning.

test_cases.description: 1 rise attempt failure — below threshold
test_cases.notes: Single non-consecutive failure. Below >= 2 consecutive threshold.

test_cases.description: 1 rise attempt failure, isolated — no trend
test_cases.notes: Single isolated failure. No route.

test_cases.description: Sudden drop posture change — IMMEDIATE ROUTE, living room
test_cases.notes: CRITICAL: sudden_drop bypasses ALL suppression. Immediate routing regardless of baseline.

test_cases.description: Sudden drop posture change — IMMEDIATE ROUTE, bathroom
test_cases.notes: CRITICAL: Bathroom fall. Immediate routing. No baseline needed.

test_cases.description: Sudden drop posture change — IMMEDIATE ROUTE, bedroom at night
test_cases.notes: CRITICAL: Nighttime bedroom fall. Immediate routing.

test_cases.description: Sudden drop posture change — IMMEDIATE ROUTE, kitchen
test_cases.notes: CRITICAL: Kitchen fall during mealtime. Immediate routing.

test_cases.description: Sudden drop — IMMEDIATE ROUTE, during learning period
test_cases.notes: CRITICAL: sudden_drop BYPASSES learning period. Routes immediately even during learning.

test_cases.description: Sudden drop — IMMEDIATE ROUTE, low confidence sensor
test_cases.notes: CRITICAL: Even with lower confidence, sudden_drop still routes immediately.

test_cases.description: Sudden drop — IMMEDIATE ROUTE, after recent alert (no 48h suppression)
test_cases.notes: CRITICAL: sudden_drop bypasses 48h suppression window. Second fall same day still routes.

test_cases.description: Sudden drop — IMMEDIATE ROUTE, hallway at dawn
test_cases.notes: CRITICAL: Dawn hallway fall. Early morning high-risk period.

test_cases.description: Sudden drop — IMMEDIATE ROUTE, entrance area
test_cases.notes: CRITICAL: Entrance area fall. Possibly returning from outside.

test_cases.description: Sudden drop — IMMEDIATE ROUTE, with concurrent slow gait data
test_cases.notes: CRITICAL: Sudden drop accompanied by slow gait. Both mobility signals present.

test_cases.description: Wandering at 01:30 AM — nighttime cognitive issue
test_cases.notes: 01:30 is within 23:00-05:00 window. Nighttime wandering triggers cognitive routing.

test_cases.description: Wandering at 03:00 AM — deep night cognitive issue
test_cases.notes: 03:00 AM wandering in kitchen. Classic nighttime confusion pattern.

test_cases.description: Wandering at 23:30 — beginning of night window
test_cases.notes: 23:30 is within 23:00-05:00 window. Routes to cognitive expert.

test_cases.description: Wandering at 04:50 — near end of night window
test_cases.notes: 04:50 is within 23:00-05:00 window. Near boundary but still in range.

test_cases.description: Wandering across two nights — multi-day cognitive concern
test_cases.notes: Two-night wandering trend. Escalating duration.

test_cases.description: Wandering at 10:00 AM daytime — outside night window
test_cases.notes: 10:00 AM is outside 23:00-05:00 window. Daytime wandering does not trigger cognitive routing.

test_cases.description: Wandering at 15:00 PM — daytime, no route
test_cases.notes: Afternoon wandering. Outside night window. No route.

test_cases.description: Wandering at 06:00 AM — outside night window
test_cases.notes: 06:00 is outside 23:00-05:00 window. Early morning but not nighttime. No route.

test_cases.description: 5 hours daytime inactivity — above threshold
test_cases.notes: 5 hours well above 4-hour threshold and far from baseline of 1.5h.

test_cases.description: Exactly 4 hours daytime inactivity — boundary
test_cases.notes: Boundary case: exactly 4 hours meets >= 4 threshold.

test_cases.description: 7 hours daytime inactivity — severe
test_cases.notes: 7 hours of daytime inactivity. Severe disengagement.

test_cases.description: Multi-day 5+ hour daytime inactivity — trend
test_cases.notes: Two-day trend of prolonged inactivity. Progressive worsening.

test_cases.description: Three-day inactivity trend ≥ 4 hours each
test_cases.notes: Three-day escalating inactivity trend. Strong cognitive concern signal.

test_cases.description: 3 hours daytime inactivity — below threshold
test_cases.notes: 3 hours below 4-hour threshold. Could be normal nap. No route.

test_cases.description: 2 hours daytime inactivity — well within normal
test_cases.notes: 2 hours within normal range. No route.

test_cases.description: Anomalous appliance interaction — microwave used for 45 min
test_cases.notes: 45 min microwave interaction is extremely anomalous (baseline avg 5 min). Possible confusion.

test_cases.description: Repeated stove interaction — 30 min anomalous
test_cases.notes: 30 min stove interaction, far above 8 min baseline. Anomalous usage pattern.

test_cases.description: Appliance interaction anomaly — washing machine left running repeatedly
test_cases.notes: Multi-day pattern of forgetting washing machine. Duration far exceeds baseline.

test_cases.description: Appliance difficulty — repeated on/off cycling of TV
test_cases.notes: 15 on/off cycles in 10 min. Appliance difficulty pattern suggesting confusion.

test_cases.description: Appliance interaction — fridge door opened 20 times in 1 hour
test_cases.notes: 20 fridge opens in 1 hour. Repetitive behavior pattern. Cognitive concern.

test_cases.description: Multi-class: bed exits + slow gait — sleep + mobility
test_cases.notes: Both sleep (3 bed exits) and mobility (37.5% slow) thresholds exceeded.

test_cases.description: Multi-class: tossing + rise failures — sleep + mobility
test_cases.notes: Poor sleep (40 min tossing) followed by morning rise failures.

test_cases.description: Multi-class: bed exits + slow gait multi-day trend
test_cases.notes: Two-day pattern of sleep disruption + gait slowdown.

test_cases.description: Multi-class: slow gait + nighttime wandering — mobility + cognitive
test_cases.notes: Both mobility (37.5% slow) and cognitive (nighttime wandering) thresholds hit.

test_cases.description: Multi-class: rise failures + inactivity — mobility + cognitive
test_cases.notes: Morning rise failures followed by prolonged inactivity. Both domains triggered.

test_cases.description: Multi-class: gait slow + appliance anomaly — mobility + cognitive
test_cases.notes: Gait 40% below baseline + anomalous appliance use. Dual-domain concern.

test_cases.description: Multi-class: tossing + nighttime wandering — sleep + cognitive
test_cases.notes: Restless sleep followed by nighttime wandering. Sleep + cognitive domains.

test_cases.description: Multi-class: bed exits + inactivity — sleep + cognitive
test_cases.notes: Night disruption + daytime disengagement. Both domains triggered.

test_cases.description: Triple-class: bed exits + slow gait + nighttime wandering
test_cases.notes: All three domains triggered: sleep (bed exits), mobility (slow gait), cognitive (nighttime wandering).

test_cases.description: Triple-class: tossing + rise failures + inactivity
test_cases.notes: Full cascade: poor sleep → morning rise difficulty → daytime withdrawal. All three domains.

test_cases.description: Suppression: single isolated bed exit — no trend
test_cases.notes: Single isolated event. No multi-day trend. Suppressed.

test_cases.description: Suppression: single slow walking event — no trend
test_cases.notes: Single-day anomaly. System requires multi-day trend (min_distinct_days: 2). Suppressed on first occurrence.

test_cases.description: Suppression: single nighttime wandering — isolated
test_cases.notes: Single isolated nighttime wandering. No multi-day trend formed yet. Suppressed.

test_cases.description: Suppression: single inactivity event — no trend
test_cases.notes: Single day inactivity above threshold. Single-event anomaly suppressed — need multi-day trend.

test_cases.description: Suppression: single appliance anomaly — isolated event
test_cases.notes: Single appliance anomaly. No multi-day trend. Suppressed.

test_cases.description: Suppression: sleep alert sent 24h ago, no worsening — duplicate suppression
test_cases.notes: Same class alert sent within 48h lookback window and not worse. Suppressed to prevent alert fatigue.

test_cases.description: Suppression: mobility alert 36h ago, same severity — suppress
test_cases.notes: Mobility alert sent 36h ago with same severity. Within 48h window, not worse. Suppressed.

test_cases.description: Suppression: cognitive alert 40h ago, same pattern — suppress
test_cases.notes: Cognitive alert sent 40h ago. Within 48h window and not escalating. Suppressed.

test_cases.description: Suppression: sleep alert 20h ago, not worse — suppress
test_cases.notes: Sleep alert sent 20h ago. Current tossing (35 min) not worse than prior (38 min). Suppressed.

test_cases.description: Suppression: mobility alert 10h ago, same level — suppress
test_cases.notes: Same severity mobility alert within 48h. 2 failures same as before. Suppressed.

test_cases.description: Not suppressed: multi-day trend forms after initial suppression
test_cases.notes: Multi-day trend established (2+ distinct days). First-time alert for this pattern. Should route.

test_cases.description: Not suppressed: worsening trend despite prior alert
test_cases.notes: Prior alert was at 0.52 m/s. Now 0.40 m/s — significantly worse. Worsening bypasses suppression.

test_cases.description: Not suppressed: prior alert > 48h ago — suppression window expired
test_cases.notes: Prior alert was > 72h ago (Jan 12). Suppression window (48h) has expired. New trend routes.

test_cases.description: Not suppressed: escalating inactivity despite prior alert 30h ago
test_cases.notes: Prior alert at 5 hours inactivity. Now 7 hours — worsening. Routes despite 30h window.

test_cases.description: Not suppressed: new multi-day tossing trend, no prior alerts
test_cases.notes: First-time two-day trend. No prior alerts. Routes normally.

test_cases.description: Learning period: normal bed exits — suppress all
test_cases.notes: Day 3 of 14-day learning period. All normal events suppressed to build baseline.

test_cases.description: Learning period: slow gait — suppress for baseline building
test_cases.notes: Day 7 of learning period. Gait data is being collected for baseline. No routing yet.

test_cases.description: Learning period: nighttime wandering — suppress for baseline
test_cases.notes: Day 10 of learning period. Even above-threshold events suppressed during learning.

test_cases.description: Learning period: sudden drop — BYPASS learning, route immediately
test_cases.notes: CRITICAL: sudden_drop BYPASSES learning period. Day 2 but still routes immediately.

test_cases.description: Learning period: sudden drop day 12 — still bypass
test_cases.notes: CRITICAL: sudden_drop BYPASSES learning period. Day 12, near end of learning but still routes.

test_cases.description: Edge case: empty event log — no route
test_cases.notes: Empty event log. Nothing to analyze. No route.

test_cases.description: Edge case: exactly 2 bed exits + exactly 30 min tossing — double boundary
test_cases.notes: Double boundary: exactly 2 bed exits AND exactly 30 min tossing. Both meet >= thresholds.

test_cases.description: Edge case: unknown event type — ignore gracefully
test_cases.notes: Unknown event types should be ignored gracefully. No error, no route.

test_cases.description: Edge case: event with missing fields — handle gracefully
test_cases.notes: Events with missing required fields (count, timestamp, duration). System should handle gracefully without crashing.

test_cases.description: Edge case: multiple sudden drops in same period — single immediate route
test_cases.notes: Multiple sudden drops in 10 minutes. Should consolidate into single immediate route to avoid duplicate alerts.
